original: Nadia Comăneci(14) becomes the first gymnast in Olympic Games history to achieve a perfect 10 score (doing so 7 times) at the Montreal Games
Comet Shoemaker-Levy's largest collision with Jupiter leaves a black spot 12,000 km across
Crayola announces the introduction of scented crayons
First human test of a vaccine against cholera; Ukrainian bacteriologist Waldemar Haffkine risks his life by successfully testing it on himself
Douglas "Wrong Way" Corrigan arrives in Ireland after a 28-hour flight, supposedly leaving New York flying for California
Videotapes released showing Titanic's sunken remains
World's first operational jet-powered fighter aircraft, Messerschmitt Me 262, makes its first test flight with jet engines
Lava from Kilauea volcano eruption has now destroyed 700 homes and added 700 acres to Big Island confirms Hawaii National Guard
New York Yankees'David Conebecomes 15th pitcher to throw a perfect game (6-0 vs Montreal)

Elvis Presleypays $3.98 to make his first demo at Sun Studio in Memphis, recording "My Happiness" and "That's When Your Heartaches Begin" and reportedly gives the record to his mother as a belated birthday present.
Inge Sørensen, Danish swimmer (youngest female Olympic Games medalist in an individual event [12] bronze 200m breaststroke 1936), born in Skovshoved, Denmark (d. 2011)
Babe Ruthachieves 139 home runs with a MLB record 575 feet hit to become the all-time home run leader in Major League Baseball, taking the title from Roger Connor
1st electric power generated from atomic energy sold commercially
IntelCorporation is founded in Santa Clara, California
June 2019 was the hottest June on record with average worldwide temperature of 61.6°F (16.4°C) according to The US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
Jerome H. Lemelson, American inventor, (held more than 600 patents), born in Staten Island, New York (d. 1997)
One of world's earliest mosques at 1,200 years old discovered by archaeologists in Israel's Negev Desert
Gemini 10 launches John Young &Michael Collins
Rohini 1, 1st Indian satellite, launches into orbit
Wildfire season in British Columbia becomes the most destructive on record, burning 14,100 square kilometers of land and surpassing the 2018 record
William 'Bill' Wright becomes the 1st African American to win a major golf tournament (U.S. Amateur Public Links Championships)
New Earth geological age announced, the Meghalayan Age 4,200 years ago to the present, by the International Commission on Stratigraphy
City of Phoenix, Arizona breaks a five decade heat record recording 19 consecutive days of temperature at or above 110°F (43.3°C); overnight low of 97°F (36.1°C) highest since 1989
World's tallest structure, a 646-meter Polish radio mast, is completed
Zond 3 launches to fly by the Moon and enters solar orbit
Gandhileaves South Africa after successfully leading campaigns of Passive Resistance
280 mm rain in Martinstown, Dorset (UK record)

HMS Investigator sets off on a voyage to determine if New Holland (Australia) is one island or two, under command ofMatthew Flinders, with botanist Robert Brown and artists Ferdinand Bauer and William Westall aboard
Thiokol conducts 2-min firing of space shuttle's SRB at Brigham, Ut
Jersey Joe WalcottKOsEzzard Charlesin 7 rounds, becoming at age 37 the then oldest world heavyweight champion, at Forbes Field in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
Ty Cobb's 4,000th MLB career hit
San Francisco Giants outfielderWillie Maysbecomes 10th baseball player to get 3,000 hits
CommissionerFord FrickrulesBabe Ruth's record of 60 HR in 154-game sched in 1927, must be broken in 1st 154 of 162 games
48 cm rainfall at Rockport, West Virginia (state record)
200,000 attend Mt Pocono rock festival in Penns
Martha Reeves, American pop and R&B singer (Martha & the Vandellas - "Dancing in the Street", "Heat Wave", "Jimmie Mack"), born in Eufaula, Alabama
Red Skelton [Richard], American comedian (Red Skelton Show), born in Vincennes, Indiana (d. 1997)
Jack Dongarra, American computer programmer (2022 Turing award, Linpack), born in Chicago, Illinois
Oskar Sala, German physicist, composer, electronic music pioneer, and Trautonium (an early synthesizer) player, born in Greiz, Principality of the Reuss Elder Line, Empire of Germany (d. 2002)
Shirley Strickland, Australian athlete (Olympic gold 80m hurdles 1952, 56; 4 x 100m relay 1956), born in Sydney, Australia (d. 2004)
Peace Pilgrim [Mildred Lisette Norman], American peace activist, born in Egg Harbor City, New Jersey (d. 1981)
Hartmut Michel, German chemist (Nobel 1988 - determination of the structure of certain proteins that are essential for photosynthesis), born in Ludwigsburg, Germany
Dion DiMucci, American rocker (Dion & Belmonts - "A Teenager In Love"), born in The Bronx, New York City
Ricky Skaggs, American country singer ("Heartbroke"; "Toy Hearts"), born in Cordell, Kentucky
Cesar Zuiderwijk, Dutch rock drummer (Golden Earring - "Radar Love"), born in The Hague, Netherlands
Ted Harris, Canadian ice hockey defenseman (5 x NHL All Star; Stanley Cup 1965, 66, 68, 69 Montreal Canadiens, 75 Philadelphia Flyers), born in Winnipeg, Canada
Gino Bartali, Italian cyclist (Tour de France 1938, 48; Giro d'Italia 1936-7, 46), born in Ponte a Ema, Florence, Italy (d. 2000)
Thomas Kuhn, American philosopher of science (paradigm shift), born in Cincinnati, Ohio (d. 1996)
Kurt Masur, German conductor (New York Philharmonic, 1991-2002; Gewandhausorchester Leipzig, 1970-96), born in Brieg, Lower Silesia, Weimar Germany (d. 2015)
Henri Salvador, French-Caribbean pop singer, guitarist, and cabaret artist, born in Cayenne, French Guiana (d. 2008)
Dudu Pukwana [Mtutuzel], South African saxophonist and composer, born in Walmer Township, Port Elizabeth, South Africa (d. 1990)
R. Murray Schafer, Canadian composer (Patria), music educator, and environmentalist, born in Sarnia, Ontario (d. 2021)
Lonnie Mack [McIntosh], American blues-rock guitarist, and singer-songwriter (Baby What's Wrong), born in West Harrison, Indiana (d. 2016)
Margaret Laurence, Canadian writer (The Stone Angel), born in Neepawa, Manitoba (d. 1987)
Aaron Beck, American psychiatrist (father of cognitive behavioural therapy), born in Providence, Rhode Island (d. 2021)[1]
Hendrik Antoon Lorentz, Dutch physicist (electromagnetic radiation, Nobel 1902), born in Arnherm, Netherlands (d. 1928)
Adelaide Tambo, South African anti-apartheid activist, politician and wife of Oliver Tambo, born in Top Location, Vereeniging (d. 2007)
Burt Kwouk, English actor (Pink Panther movies), born in Warrington (d. 2016)
Stephen Gill Spottswood, American African Methodist Episcopal Zion bishop, and civil rights leader (NAACP Chairman, 1961-74), born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 1974)
Barry Gray [John Livesey Eccles], British pianist, ondist, Hammond organ player, film and television composer (Thunderbirds; Space:1999), born in Blackburn, Lancashire, England (d. 1984)
